proposed_from.topic: completeness of the SS-ID algorithm at its open cs_fail_2 failure line for data fusion under systematic selection (Lee-Ghassami-Shpitser, arXiv:2404.06602, UAI 2024): define the context-specific hedge (CS-hedge) as a graph-only correlation-hiding structure over label-set ADMG multigraphs, and prove the context-marginal sufficiency lemma — every admissible context U-severs one side, so each context law depends only on the uncoupled per-side marginals while the target Q = p(Y(a), S=empty), living in the un-severed S=empty world, depends on the coupled sum kappa — hence SS-ID is sound and complete, settling the authors' published conjecture
proposed_from.novelty_justification: The focal object is a label-respecting, whole-arm correlation-hiding certificate. An ordinary hedge hides a contrast in one observational law, and hedgelets or thickets preserve separately supplied experiments. Neither provides a selector-context condition under which the exact conditional laws \(p(V^\circ\mid S=u)\) erase one member of a coupled latent pair while \(p(Y(a),S=\emptyset)\) retains their joint Fourier coefficient.
seed_list: Every terminal SS-ID confounded-selector failure contains a CS-hedge that witnesses nonidentification of \(p(Y(a),S=\emptyset)\).
A label-aware two-block hedge hides its coupling from every supplied context law exactly when each nonempty context severs one block.
The parity witness for a CS-hedge can be made full-support on a relative-open family of compatible context laws.
Binary response coordinates suffice for every terminal SS-ID failure, or a minimal labelled multigraph forces a larger alphabet.
The terminal SS-ID trace admits a finite linear response-function null direction if and only if its context restrictions form a CS-hedge.
A selector-indexed thicket is complete for failures outside the confounded-selector branch but cannot certify the terminal branch without context severing.
Positivity-compatible gID decompositions remain incomplete after systematic selection unless their local hedges are context-severing.
Transportability s-hedges cease to be valid under experiment-assignment selection precisely when a supplied context retains both parity blocks.

seed_details.one_line: Every terminal SS-ID confounded-selector failure contains a CS-hedge that witnesses nonidentification of \(p(Y(a),S=\emptyset)\).

anchor_paper.author: Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser
anchor_paper.claim: SS-ID is sound and the authors conjecture its completeness after proving nonidentification for only specified failures.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: We conjecture this algorithm is also complete.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not a definitional iff: the converse must construct a label-uniform countermodel family.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from ordinary hedges because the selector can be treated as another observed node.
seed_details.rebuttal: That reduction fails because every supplied selector context must have the same law, while the target is evaluated in the unselected context.
seed_details.strict_delta: The new condition quantifies edge severing jointly over all supplied label contexts; no ordinary hedge or thicket contains this requirement.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: Passes: setting \(\mathcal U=\emptyset\) removes the new quantifier and recovers, rather than proves, the classical result.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only sufficiency of the new certificate is credible.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The converse is explicitly retained as Open-ended Question 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: The construction does not name every context law.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Context-law equivalence is formalized by \(M_0\equiv_{\mathcal U}M_1\).

adversarial_referee.instance: A single zero-probability witness is not graph-level.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The core claims finite compatibility only; generic positivity is not claimed.

seed_details.burned_reason: The universal terminal CS-hedge and exact two-block context-severing claims were not established; the attempted witness was refuted by forced selector-edge label deletion.

seed_details.one_line: A label-aware two-block hedge hides its coupling from every supplied context law exactly when each nonempty context severs one block.

anchor_paper.author: Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser
anchor_paper.claim: Systematic selection is represented through context-specific restrictions of a label-set multigraph.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the corresponding graph can be recovered by checking for each edge whether the label intersects the value of \(S\).

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The focal object is a graph-level correlation-hiding certificate, not a restatement of do-calculus.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only the ordinary parity construction performed conditionally on \(S\).
seed_details.rebuttal: Conditional equality in one context does not imply equality across the entire supplied family; the uniform severing lemma is the missing invariant.

adversarial_referee.instance: No new identifying functional is offered.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The claim is an SCM nonidentification certificate, not point identification.

adversarial_referee.instance: Severing may be too strong to characterize failure.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The theorem claims sufficiency; exactness is reserved for Open-ended Question 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: The paper proves the relevant failure already.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Its Theorem 5 excludes the terminal return after the context search fails.

seed_details.burned_reason: The universal terminal CS-hedge and exact two-block context-severing claims were not established; the attempted witness was refuted by forced selector-edge label deletion.

seed_details.one_line: The parity witness for a CS-hedge can be made full-support on a relative-open family of compatible context laws.

anchor_paper.author: Kivva et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Positivity is essential to soundness and completeness in general identifiability.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: adding the assumption will cause the completeness proof ... to fail.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: A relative-open positive family would avoid the single-boundary-witness defect.
seed_details.referee_objection: The positivity perturbation is mechanical once a null direction exists.
seed_details.rebuttal: The unresolved point is whether every context equality leaves a target-changing direction after strict support constraints.

seed_details.one_line: Binary response coordinates suffice for every terminal SS-ID failure, or a minimal labelled multigraph forces a larger alphabet.

anchor_paper.author: Shpitser and Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Classical hedge nonidentification has a binary finite countermodel construction.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The question is an alphabet frontier, not a routine binary encoding claim.
seed_details.referee_objection: Alphabet enlargement is a technicality with no identification consequence.
seed_details.rebuttal: A universal binary theorem would make the proposed certificate finitely constructive; a counterexample would expose a real limitation.

seed_details.one_line: The terminal SS-ID trace admits a finite linear response-function null direction if and only if its context restrictions form a CS-hedge.

anchor_paper.author: Lee, Correa, and Bareinboim
anchor_paper.claim: Hedgelets and thickets yield a complete nonidentification construction for arbitrary surrogate experiments.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: failure of the algorithm ... implies that the target effect is not computable.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The null-direction representation is meaningful only if it remains graph-readable.
seed_details.referee_objection: Response-function rank is exponential and simply restates nonidentification.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is retained as a construction diagnostic, not the chosen headline, because the graph-to-rank converse is uncertain.

seed_details.one_line: A selector-indexed thicket is complete for failures outside the confounded-selector branch but cannot certify the terminal branch without context severing.

anchor_paper.author: Lee, Correa, and Bareinboim
anchor_paper.claim: Thickets synchronize hedges across a collection of supplied experimental distributions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: a graphical structure ... relative to the available input distributions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: This is a nonrepresentability diagnostic, not a new complete algorithm.
seed_details.referee_objection: It follows directly by indexing gID inputs with selector values.
seed_details.rebuttal: That indexing omits the selector's context-specific structural restrictions, which are the obstacle in the terminal branch.

seed_details.one_line: Positivity-compatible gID decompositions remain incomplete after systematic selection unless their local hedges are context-severing.

anchor_paper.author: Kivva et al.
anchor_paper.claim: A repaired positive-law algorithm is sound and complete for general identifiability.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: we present a new algorithm that is provably both sound and complete.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The selection mechanism adds structural context restrictions absent from gID.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is merely gID after augmenting the graph with \(S\).
seed_details.rebuttal: Augmentation does not preserve the supplied context laws or their context-specific interventions.

seed_details.one_line: Transportability s-hedges cease to be valid under experiment-assignment selection precisely when a supplied context retains both parity blocks.

anchor_paper.author: Bareinboim and Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Selection diagrams admit a complete transportability characterization.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The object differs from a population-shift s-hedge through context-specific experiment assignment.
seed_details.referee_objection: The criterion is terminology transfer from s-hedges.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed criterion is only a comparison diagnostic and is dominated by the direct SS-ID seed.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser formulate CS-SCMs for data fusion under systematic selection, prove SS-ID soundness, and state a completeness conjecture after proving nonidentification at Algorithm 1 lines 4 and 14 and Algorithm 2 line 3.
Their terminal confounded-selector return, after no candidate context passes its independence and reachability test, is not covered by their nonidentification theorem.
Their Appendix B.2 gives label-set ADMG multigraph semantics, so a certificate must be invariant under each context restriction rather than under one fixed graph.
Shpitser and Pearl give the ordinary hedge and binary parity countermodels for classical ID.
Huang and Valtorta independently establish ordinary do-calculus completeness.
Lee, Correa, and Bareinboim construct hedgelets and thickets to preserve a family of supplied experimental laws and prove gID completeness.
Kivva et al. show that gID completeness changes under positivity, warning against an unexamined transfer of old countermodels.
Bareinboim and Pearl give transportability completeness with selection diagrams, but not selector-indexed experimental contexts.
The prior in-repository proposal named an overlap hedge but did not establish context-uniform coupling invisibility.
The surviving kernel is therefore the CS-hedge plus the context-marginal lemma, with the exhaustive terminal-branch converse held as the genuine open content.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1 / Theorem 2 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Universal extraction from the remaining terminal trace is not assumed proved; the parity result and resulting completeness frontier are restricted to finite binary, label-coherent CS-SCM inputs with positive supplied and target selector mass.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: If Open-ended Question 1 is answered affirmatively, SS-ID succeeds if and only if Q is identifiable on the stated finite-binary positive-support CS-SCM domain.

banked_reason: D0.5 rejected the headline: the proposed terminal trace violates mandatory LS-ADMMG label deletion, the prompt uses the wrong SS-ID target semantics, and no universal cs_fail_2 certificate was proved.
